text.title: Multi-Agent Design Pattern Selection Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that constrain pattern picks across the 8-canonical set.

rule.statement: A multi-agent system MUST declare exactly ONE primary pattern from the 8-canonical set in its design.md. Stacking ("Hierarchical + Loop + HITL") is forbidden; nest patterns explicitly (e.g. Hierarchical contains a Loop sub-graph for one worker).
rule.rationale: One named pattern keeps the decision auditable. Stacks hide the actual shape and make pattern-specific failure modes invisible to reviewers.

rule.statement: The decision record MUST link to the downstream impl methodology: Sequential / Parallel → `multi-agent-basics`; Hierarchical → `multi-agent-hierarchical`; Generator-Critic / Loop → `multi-agent-conversational`; Blackboard → `multi-agent-collaborative`; Router / HITL → `multi-agent-production-bus`.
rule.rationale: Without a downstream link the decision is academic; implementations drift from intent.

rule.statement: Blackboard pattern selection REQUIRES an explicit concurrency-control plan (single-writer queue, CRDT, or DB transactions). Without it the choice is silently incorrect.
rule.rationale: Blackboard means agents share mutable state; concurrent writes without coordination produce non-deterministic results.

rule.statement: Router pattern selection REQUIRES a documented classifier with measured accuracy (≥0.85 on a held-out test set) or a deterministic rule set. A router that hand-waves the routing decision is just an orchestrator on cheap labels.
rule.rationale: Router pattern only pays off if the routing decision is cheap AND accurate; a fuzzy classifier degrades to broadcast or worse.

rule.statement: Every irreversible action in the design (DB writes, payments, external emails, deploys) MUST be gated by Human-in-the-Loop OR a typed approval check. Selecting any other primary pattern does NOT exempt the design from this gate.
rule.rationale: Irreversible actions on autopilot are the dominant production failure mode for any multi-agent system regardless of pattern.
